MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire

Serving as Conservative MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire since May 2010.
Served as Minister of State at the Department for Transport October 2022 to November 2023 and as Financial Secretary to the Treasury May 2019 to September 2021. Appointed Shadow Leader of the House of Commons in November 2024.

MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire

Serving as Conservative MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire since May 2010.
Served as Minister of State at the Department for Transport October 2022 to November 2023 and as Financial Secretary to the Treasury May 2019 to September 2021. Appointed Shadow Leader of the House of Commons in November 2024.

MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire

Serving as Conservative MP for Hereford and South Herefordshire since May 2010.
Served as Minister of State at the Department for Transport October 2022 to November 2023 and as Financial Secretary to the Treasury May 2019 to September 2021. Appointed Shadow Leader of the House of Commons in November 2024.

In Pictures
Raising awareness of water pollution

Local MP Jesse Norman is backing a joint effort by Ross Rowing Club and local firm Haigh Environmental to raise awareness of the issue of water pollution in the River Wye, as well as the solutions available to tackle it.
Government action on River Wye “just the first step”, says Jesse

Local MP and long time River Wye campaigner Jesse Norman has given a qualified welcome to news today of the first joined-up action by governments on the river.
Jesse Norman MP addresses the UK Youth Parliament

Local MP Jesse Norman was a guest speaker at the opening of the United Kingdom Youth Parliament 2025 last week.
News
Article for the Hereford Times - Hereford in Bloom, and in Spirit
6 November 2025

I am delighted at the wonderful recent news that Hereford has won Gold in the RHS Heart of England in Bloom Awards for 2025. 
Article for the Hereford Times - National Hospice Week
9 October 2025

This week is National Hospice Week, and it could not come at a more opportune time. Across the country, the UK’s hospice movement is under strain as rarely if ever before. 
Article for the Hereford Times - Northern Ireland veterans
17 July 2025

On Monday, I had the honour of taking part in one of the most extraordinary events of my 15 years in Parliament — a debate on Northern Ireland veterans and the Legacy Act.
All News
In Parliament
Jesse Norman welcomes report on access to Parliament and its procedures
16 April 2026

Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman welcomes the Modernisation Committee’s report on access to the House of Commons and its Procedures and the administration’s response.
Jesse Norman demands apology for PM’s contempt of Parliament and grills Government on delayed Defence Investment Plan
16 April 2026

Shadow Leader of the House Jesse Norman highlights the Prime Minister's contemptuous treatment of the Speaker  and demands an apology to him and to the House, and he presses the Leader of the House on the fate of the Defence Investment Plan, which he promised WILL be published.
Jesse Norman urges Government action on Wiggin pension injustice to force trustees to pay top-ups from surpluses
19 March 2026

Jesse Norman urges the Government to intervene in the longstanding injustice perpetrated on pensioners of the Special Metals Wiggin pension fund, by requiring the trustees to use the scheme’s surpluses to provide top-up payments to those affected.
